source: IITA - International Institute of Tropical Agriculture

facts.text: Plough and harrow the field two to three weeks before planting. Level the field well so water spreads evenly across the plot.
Use improved varieties such as FARO 44 or FARO 52 for higher yields and resistance to common pests.
Transplant seedlings at 20cm by 20cm spacing. Use two to three seedlings per hole.
Apply NPK at planting and top-dress with urea at tillering and panicle initiation stages.
Watch for dead hearts in young plants. Remove and destroy affected stems and keep the field weed free.
